relevant_scenes: 0-14 (00:00-05:55). Scenes 15-74 (05:56-07:59) are a GMass YouTube mid-roll ad — discarded.

modules.name: Auto Dialer
modules.description: Outbound calling automation module. Contains campaign management and record list management. Accessible via top navigation 'Auto dialer' tab.

modules.name: Reports
modules.description: Analytics and performance reporting for outbound campaigns. Shows campaign metrics, agent sessions, dispositions and call details.

features.name: Campaign Management
features.description: Create, manage and monitor outbound calling campaigns. Campaigns can be in states: Running, Ready, Completed, Paused, Incomplete.
user_steps: Navigate to Auto Dialer > Campaigns
Click 'Create campaign' button
Fill in General Settings (name, description, timezone, dates, business hours, phone numbers)
Configure Dialer Settings (choose dialer mode and mode-specific parameters)
Add agents to Agent List
Upload or select a Record List
Save and launch campaign
features.business_value: Enables teams to run structured outbound calling campaigns with full control over scheduling, staffing, and contact lists.

features.name: Predictive Dialing Mode
features.description: Algorithm-driven mass calling for large teams. Maximizes call efficiency and reduces agent idle time. At campaign start, algorithm collects statistics to make accurate predictions. Dials multiple contacts simultaneously.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings, select 'Predictive' mode
Enable/disable Auto Answer
Set Timeout (seconds before auto-answer)
Configure Timing: Ringing agent time, Wrap-up time, Max ring time
Set Dialing Attempts: Default retry period, Max attempts per record
Set Performance Metrics: Calls per agent (Auto or manual), Abandon rate %
Enable Answering Machine Detection
Configure System Disposition actions (Retry/Final per outcome)
Configure Agent Disposition options (which outcomes agents can select)
features.business_value: Maximizes agent productivity for large outbound teams by minimizing idle time between calls.

features.name: Preview Dialing Mode
features.description: Agent is shown contact details before the call is placed. Agent manually reviews and initiates the call. Best for small teams requiring manual review.
user_steps: Select 'Preview' mode in Dialer Settings
features.business_value: Gives agents context before calling, improving conversation quality for high-value or complex contacts.

features.name: Progressive Dialing Mode
features.description: System dials one call per available agent, maintaining steady flow. Best for small to medium teams.
user_steps: Select 'Progressive' mode in Dialer Settings
features.business_value: Balances automation with control — no abandoned calls, steady agent workload.

features.name: Agentless Dialing Mode
features.description: Automated outbound calling without live agents. Suitable for IVR-based campaigns, surveys, or broadcast messages.
user_steps: Select 'Agentless' mode in Dialer Settings
features.business_value: Enables fully automated outreach at scale without requiring agent staffing.

features.name: Local Presence
features.description: Automatically selects the best outbound business number based on the contact's area code. Makes calls appear local to the prospect, increasing answer rates.
user_steps: In General Settings, assign multiple business numbers with regional labels
Toggle on 'Local Presence'
System automatically matches caller ID to contact area code at call time
features.business_value: Increases answer rates by displaying a local number to prospects instead of an unknown toll-free or out-of-state number.

features.name: Business Hours Scheduling
features.description: Configure per-day calling windows for a campaign. Each day (Sunday–Saturday) can be enabled/disabled with custom AM/PM start and end times, or set to Full Day.
user_steps: In General Settings, scroll to Business Hours section
Enable/disable each day with checkbox
Set start and end times per day
Or toggle 'Full day' for unrestricted daily calling
features.business_value: Ensures calls are placed only during legally and operationally acceptable hours, applied per the campaign timezone.

features.name: National DNC List Scrubber
features.description: Upload a Do Not Call list to prevent dialing numbers on the national DNC registry. Integrated into the Campaigns screen.
user_steps: On Campaigns screen, locate 'National DNC list scrubber' panel (top right)
Download template if needed
Upload your DNC list CSV
features.business_value: Ensures compliance with TCPA and national DNC regulations, protecting the business from legal liability.

features.name: Answering Machine Detection (AMD)
features.description: Automatically detects when a call reaches an answering machine/voicemail. System can be configured to retry or treat as final based on AMD result.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings (Predictive mode), toggle on 'Answering machine detection'
In System Disposition, configure AMD outcome (Retry with period / Final)
features.business_value: Prevents wasting agent time on voicemails and enables intelligent retry logic.

features.name: System Disposition
features.description: Automated handling rules for call outcomes not controlled by agents (busy, no response, AMD, invalid number, robocall block). Each outcome can be set to Retry (with period and attempt limits) or Final.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings, scroll to System Disposition section
For each outcome (Contact busy, Contact no response, Agent abandoned, Invalid number, Answering machine, Blocked by robocall analytics): choose Retry or Final
For Retry: set retry period and max attempts per number
features.business_value: Automates retry logic and eliminates manual queue management for unreachable contacts.

features.name: Agent Disposition
features.description: Configurable set of call outcome options that agents select after each call. Each disposition can be marked as Final (removes from queue) or non-Final (remains eligible for retry). Visible options per campaign are configurable.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings, scroll to Agent Disposition section
Enable or disable specific disposition options for this campaign
Mark each as Final or not (Final = no more retries)
features.business_value: Standardizes agent call outcomes, drives retry logic, and feeds reporting/analytics.

features.name: Record List Management
features.description: Upload and manage contact lists (CSV format) for use in campaigns. MightyCall auto-maps CSV fields. Lists show record count, unique area codes, and coverage score.
user_steps: In campaign creation Step 4 (Record list), upload a CSV file or drag-and-drop
Or select an existing record list from the right panel
Download the template for correct CSV format
Note: files over 15MB take 5-7 minutes to upload
Review coverage score after list is attached
features.business_value: Enables targeted outreach by attaching specific contact databases to each campaign.

features.name: Coverage Score
features.description: A percentage metric showing how many unique contact area codes in the record list are covered by the campaign's assigned local business numbers. Displayed per record list and on the Campaigns list screen.
user_steps: Attach a record list to a campaign
Assign local business numbers in General Settings
Coverage Score auto-calculates and displays in the Record List step and Campaigns table
features.business_value: Helps managers optimize local presence coverage to maximize answer rates across different geographic regions.

features.name: Dialing Attempts Configuration
features.description: Control how many times the system retries each record and the minimum time between retries. Configured within Predictive dialer mode settings.
features.business_value: Balances contact penetration with compliance and contact experience — avoids over-dialing a single number.

features.name: Campaign Analytics & Reporting
features.description: Performance dashboard for all campaigns. Shows key outbound metrics across all campaigns with filtering by date range.
user_steps: Navigate to Reports
Select date range filter
View Campaigns tab for campaign-level metrics
Use Columns button to show/hide metrics
Export data as needed
features.business_value: Provides managers visibility into campaign performance to optimize dialer settings, abandon rates, and agent efficiency.

screens.name: Campaigns List
screens.purpose: Overview of all outbound campaigns with status, agents, records, and coverage score. Entry point for campaign management.

components.action: Opens campaign creation wizard

components.label: Campaigns table

components.action: Shows Running / Ready / Completed / Paused / Incomplete per campaign

components.action: Start or pause a campaign inline

screens.name: Campaign General Settings (Step 1)
screens.purpose: Configure campaign name, description, timezone, date range, business hours, and assigned phone numbers including local presence.

components.action: Enter campaign title

components.label: Description (Optional)
components.action: Enter campaign description, 200 char limit

components.action: Set campaign start date (optional)

components.label: End date
components.action: Set campaign end date (optional)

components.action: Enable/disable each day and set AM/PM start-end times or Full Day

components.action: Assign phone numbers to the campaign with labels (LA Main, LA Sales, NYC, Toronto, San Diego, Toll-Free)

components.label: Auto-rotation
components.action: Enable number rotation

components.label: Local Presence
components.action: Auto-select best number based on contact area code

components.label: Cancel

components.label: Save
components.action: Save campaign progress

screens.name: Campaign Dialer Settings (Step 2)
screens.purpose: Select dialing mode (Preview, Progressive, Predictive, Agentless) and configure mode-specific parameters including timing, retry logic, AMD, and disposition options.

components.label: Dialer mode selector
components.action: Choose Preview / Progressive / Predictive / Agentless

components.label: Auto answer
components.action: Enable automatic call pickup by agents

components.label: Timeout (sec)
components.action: Seconds before auto-answer triggers

components.label: Ringing agent time (sec)
components.action: How long agent phone rings

components.label: Wrap-up time (sec)
components.action: Post-call wrap time before next call

components.label: Max ring time (sec)
components.action: Maximum time to let contact phone ring

components.label: Default retry period (min)
components.action: Minimum wait before retrying a record

components.label: Max attempts per record
components.action: Maximum total dial attempts per contact

components.label: Calls per agent
components.action: Set simultaneous calls per agent (Auto or manual)

components.label: Abandon rate (%)
components.action: Maximum acceptable abandoned call percentage

components.label: Answering machine detection
components.action: Enable AMD

components.label: System disposition
components.action: Set Retry/Final rules for system-level outcomes (busy, no response, AMD, invalid, blocked)

components.label: Agent disposition

screens.purpose: Upload a new CSV contact list or select an existing record list to use for this campaign. Shows list stats including record count, unique area codes, and coverage score.

components.action: Browse and select pre-uploaded lists showing record count and unique area codes

components.label: New record list
components.action: Create a new list rather than reusing existing

components.label: Coverage Score
components.action: Shows % of contact area codes covered by assigned local numbers

screens.purpose: Analytics dashboard showing performance metrics for all outbound campaigns over a selected date range.

components.label: Date range filter
components.action: Filter report by date range with preset options (last 30 days, etc.)

components.label: Report tabs
components.action: Switch between Campaigns / Dispositions / Agent sessions / Call details

components.label: Campaign metrics table
components.action: Shows all campaigns with KPI columns

components.label: Columns

components.label: Export
components.action: Export report data

user_flows.name: Create a Predictive Dialer Campaign
user_flows.description: End-to-end flow for setting up a new outbound predictive dialing campaign from scratch.

steps.action: Enter name, description, timezone, start/end dates
Assign phone numbers and enable Local Presence
Set timing (wrap-up, ring, ringing agent time) and dialing attempts
Set abandon rate and calls per agent
Enable AMD and configure system dispositions
Configure agent disposition options and mark Final/non-Final
Upload CSV or select existing record list

business_processes.name: Outbound Predictive Dialing Campaign
business_processes.description: End-to-end business process for running a predictive dialing outbound sales or outreach campaign.

steps.name: Configure predictive dialer mode and parameters
Validate coverage score for local presence
Contact answers — system connects to available agent
System applies retry/final logic based on disposition
System retries or removes record from queue
Manager reviews campaign analytics in Reports

business_processes.name: DNC Compliance Management
business_processes.description: Process for ensuring outbound calls comply with Do Not Call regulations.

steps.name: Upload National DNC list to MightyCall
System scrubs campaign record lists against DNC

data_entities.name: Campaign
data_entities.description: An outbound calling campaign with configuration, staffing, scheduling and contact list.

data_entities.name: Dialer Settings
data_entities.description: Configuration for how the auto dialer behaves within a campaign.

fields.name: max_attempts_per_record
fields.description: Max total dial attempts per contact

fields.name: calls_per_agent
fields.description: Simultaneous calls per agent (Auto or integer)

data_entities.name: System Disposition
data_entities.description: Automated outcome handling rule for a specific system-level call result.

fields.name: outcome_type
fields.description: Contact busy / Contact no response / Agent abandoned / Invalid number / Answering machine / Blocked by robocall analytics

data_entities.name: Agent Disposition
data_entities.description: Call outcome option that an agent can select after completing a call. Drives retry and reporting logic.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Display name (e.g. Successful call, Call back later, Not interested)

fields.name: is_final
fields.description: If true, contact is removed from dial queue

fields.name: is_enabled
fields.description: Whether this option is visible to agents in this campaign

data_entities.name: Record List
data_entities.description: A CSV-based contact list uploaded for use in one or more campaigns.

fields.name: unique_area_codes
fields.description: Number of distinct area codes in the list

fields.name: coverage_score_pct
fields.description: % of area codes covered by assigned local numbers

data_entities.name: Business Number
data_entities.description: A phone number assigned to a campaign for outbound caller ID, optionally labeled by region.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Regional label (e.g. LA Main, LA Sales, NYC, Toronto, San Diego, Toll-Free)

data_entities.name: Campaign Metrics (Report)
data_entities.description: Aggregated performance data per campaign over a reporting period.

ui_patterns.layout_type: Top navigation bar with dropdown sub-menus. Wizard/stepper for campaign creation (4 steps in left sidebar). Two-column layout on creation forms (settings left, supporting panel right).
ui_patterns.color_scheme: Dark navy/black top nav. White content area. Green accent (#00CC00 lime) for active nav items and brand elements. Blue action buttons. Status badges with color coding (green=Running, yellow=Ready/Incomplete, grey=Completed, orange=Paused).
ui_patterns.component_library: Custom design system. Uses card panels, toggle switches, data tables with sortable columns, step-wizard sidebar, date pickers, AM/PM time inputs.
common_patterns: 4-step campaign creation wizard with left sidebar progress tracker
Right-side resource panel (numbers, record lists) alongside main form
Inline status badges on list screens
Disposition tables with Retry/Final radio selectors and retry period inputs
Coverage score metric linking local numbers to contact area codes
Agent availability status indicator top-right (green dot + Available + timer)
